rulings.Case Title: SOMALIA FOLASHADE ET AL VS. THE SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 11, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES, LLC's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of any actionable cause of action against the moving defendant. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCANS FOR SPORTS AND RECREATION VS. ARE-SAN FRANCISCO NO. 53, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 11, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCANS FOR SPORTS AND RECREATION's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS DENIED. It cannot be determined as a matter of law that paragraph 4.8 of the agreement requires consent of the plaintiff for defendant's transfer of title to the property. Consent is clearly required as to "this Agreement, any rights or duties expressed herein," but not as to title, making this provision susceptible to multiple interpretations. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH STEWART, TRUSTEE OF THE ROBERTA L. VS. MICHAEL STEWART ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 11, 2022 line 4. PLAINTIFF KENNETH STEWART, TRUSTEE OF THE ROBERTA L. STEWART REVOCABLE TRUST'S Motion To Compel Responses GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide complete verified responses without objections for Form Interrogatories UD and General, Special Interrogatories and Request for Production within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $810. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CARTIAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. JAMIE NEUMANN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Judgment, Set Aside Default And Recall Writ Of Possession And Motion; Memo Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Nima Mehranfar; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 11, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT JAMIE NEUMANN'S Motion To Vacate Judgment, Set Aside Default And Recall Writ Of Possession And Motion Hearing Required to address service of the motion. Defendant and Peter With to be present in person or via Zoom with video capability. Defendant to address 20-day delay in seeking to vacate the default. The Court notes that a regularly noticed motion could have been calendared had Defendant promptly filed his motion. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
